mission.title: cloudevents: AsyncAPI v3 Trait for CloudEvents
mission.description: AsyncAPI v3 Trait for CloudEvents. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current cloudevents setup
steps.description:
Verify your cloudevents version:
CloudEvents is a specification — check your SDK version (e.g., pip show cloudevents)
This feature requires cloudevents as a dependency.

steps.title: Review cloudevents configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant cloudevents configuration:
I am creating Kafka APIs in AsyncAPI. These APIs should have CloudEvents headers.
Now, I was wondering if it would make sense to have an official CloudEvents Trait in AsyncAPI to include these CloudEvents headers.

steps.title: Define CloudEvents trait in AsyncAPI v3
steps.description:
AsyncAPI v3 supports CloudEvents through message traits. Define a CloudEvents trait in your AsyncAPI document:
```yaml
asyncapi: 3.0.0
info:
  title: CloudEvents Example
  version: 1.0.0
channels:
  userSignedUp:
    messages:
      userSignedUpMessage:
        traits:
          - $ref: '#/components/messageTraits/cloudEventsV1'
        payload:
          $ref: '#/components/schemas/userSignedUpData'
components:
  messageTraits:
    cloudEventsV1:
      headers:
        type: object
        properties:
          ce-specversion:
            type: string
            const: "1.0"
          ce-type:
            type: string
          ce-source:
            type: string
            format: uri-reference
          ce-id:
            type: string
        required:
          - ce-specversion
          - ce-type
          - ce-source
          - ce-id
```
This trait defines the required CloudEvents context attributes as message headers (binary content mode). Apply it to any message that should conform to the CloudEvents specification.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "AsyncAPI v3 Trait for CloudEvents" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: The AsyncAPI v3 specification includes native support for CloudEvents through its trait system. AsyncAPI v3 traits can define CloudEvents bindings, allowing AsyncAPI documents to describe event-driven APIs that use CloudEvents format. See the AsyncAPI CloudEvents binding specification for implementation details.

prerequisites.description: CloudEvents is a specification — check your SDK version (e.g., pip show cloudevents)
